rules.name: Rule of Thirds
rules.description: Place key elements along the lines or intersections of a 3x3 grid.
rules.application: Position hero text and CTAs at intersection points for visual balance.
rules.example: Hero headline positioned at the left-third vertical line.
rules.anti_pattern: Centering everything, resulting in a static, unengaging composition.

rules.name: Visual Weight Balance
rules.description: Distribute visual weight evenly to avoid a lopsided composition.
rules.application: Balance heavy elements (images, dark colors) with lighter counterparts.
rules.example: Large image on the left balanced by a text block and CTA on the right.
rules.anti_pattern: All heavy elements clustered on one side with empty space on the other.

rules.name: Directional Flow
rules.description: Guide the viewer's eye through the design in a deliberate sequence.
rules.application: Use arrows, lines, whitespace, and element sizing to create flow.
rules.example: A landing page flowing from headline to features to social proof to CTA.
rules.anti_pattern: A scattered layout where the eye bounces randomly between elements.

rules.name: Figure-Ground Relationship
rules.description: Create clear distinction between foreground content and background.
rules.application: Use elevation (shadows), color contrast, or blur to separate layers.
rules.example: A modal dialog elevated above a dimmed background overlay.
rules.anti_pattern: A popup that blends into the background with no visual separation.

rules.name: Repetition and Rhythm
rules.description: Repeat visual elements to create patterns that unify the design.
rules.application: Use consistent spacing, shapes, and colors to establish rhythm.
rules.example: A features grid where each card uses the same icon style and spacing.
rules.anti_pattern: Each feature card having completely different styling and proportions.

rules.name: Scale and Proportion
rules.description: Use deliberate size relationships to establish importance.
rules.application: Make primary content proportionally larger than supporting content.
rules.example: Hero headline 3x larger than body text; hero image 50% of viewport.
rules.anti_pattern: All elements roughly the same size, creating visual monotony.

rules.name: Negative Space as Design Element
rules.description: Treat empty space as an active composition tool, not wasted area.
rules.application: Use generous negative space to create focus and sophistication.
rules.example: A luxury brand landing page with ample whitespace around each element.
rules.anti_pattern: Filling every pixel with content, creating visual clutter.
